key_insight: PPE is an interactive predictor-corrector framework that navigates the Pareto front of deep multi-task models while respecting user preferences and the front's geometry.
engineering_relevance: It offers a practical way to tune trade-offs in deep learning without repeated costly re-optimization across many weighted-sum trials.
method: Predict along the Pareto manifold tangentially in the direction of the decision maker's preference.
Correct each predicted point to a new Pareto-optimal trade-off.
Use a Krylov subspace method with automatic differentiation to avoid explicit Hessian computations.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports validation on both toy problems and deep learning examples, showing functionality and performance in practice.
watch_item: As an interactive method, PPE may require careful choice of preference inputs and reliable matrix-vector products to remain stable and efficient.
paper_title: Interactive Pareto navigation for deep multi-task learning
